name: FFL La Surprise (K 292)
events: 16 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Monnow (Cdr. E.G. Skinner, DSC, RD, RCNR) and later with HMS Havelock (Lt.Cdr. R. Hart, DSC and Bar, RN), La Surprise (?). (1)

21 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMCS Teme (T/Lt.Cdr. D.G. Jeffrey, DSO, RCNR) and HMS Jed (Lt.Cdr. R.S. Miller, DSC, RNR) and later with HMS Highlander (Cdr. D.A. Rayner, DSC, RNVR) and La Surprise (?).
HMS United also carried out a practice attack on HMS Highlander. (1)

22 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Highlander (Cdr. D.A. Rayner, DSC, RNVR) and La Surprise (?) and later with HMS Loch Fada (Lt.Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR) and HMS Jed (Lt.Cdr. R.S. Miller, DSC, RNR). (1)

27 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Jed (Lt.Cdr. R.S. Miller, DSC, RNR) and La Surprise (?) and later with HMCS Teme (T/Lt.Cdr. D.G. Jeffrey, DSO, RCNR) and HMS Loch Fada (Lt.Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR).
HMS United also made a practice attack on HMCS Teme. (1)

8 Jun 1944 Shortly after 1600 B/8, HMS Nelson (Capt. A.H. Maxwell-Hyslop, AM, RN) departed Plymouth for Portsmouth. On departure from harbour first some exercises were carried out during which the frigate La Découverte was in company as escort. The frigate was however ordered to return to harbour around 1640 B/8.
HMS Nelson was then joined around 1730 B/8, by the frigate La Surprise and ten minutes later the escort destroyer HMS Hambledon (Lt. L.G. Toone, RN) also joined.
Around 1838 B/8, the frigates HMS Rowley (Lt.Cdr. F.J.G. Jones, RD, RNR) and HMS Holmes (Lt. D.B.G. Dumas, RN) took over the escort from HMS Hambledon and La Surprise.
They arrived at Portsmouth (The Solent) shortly before midnight. (2)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
